rows.text_1::eng: Food is anything that people eat or drink to provide the body with energy and nutrients.
rows.text_2::eng: Food helps the body grow, stay strong, and remain healthy.

rows.text_1::eng: Nutrition is the process of obtaining and providing the body with the required nutrients necessary for growth and development.
rows.image_text::eng: Good nutrition is achieved by eating a balanced diet that includes a variety of different foods.

rows.text_1::eng: Value addition is the process of improving a product to make it better, last longer, and sell at a higher price.
rows.image_text::eng: For example, as this picture demonstrates, you can transform a bottle of raw milk into a tub of yogurt, adding significant value through superior preservation and a much to higher market price, boosting your income.

rows.text_1::eng: Farmers produce different types of food that the body needs to stay healthy and strong.
rows.text_2::eng: These foods play different roles in the body, so it is important to eat a variety of them. Here are some of the examples:

rows.section_1_text::eng:
These food provides the body with the strength needed to carry out daily activities such as farming, walking, and working. 
Eat them regularly to keep your energy high.
rows.section_1_extra_text::eng:
* Maize
* Rice 
* Cassava 
* Sweet potatoes
*  Millet
* Sorghum, wheat
* Irish potatoes
* Cassava
* Arrow roots
* Yams
* Sweet potatoes
* Green bananas
* Sugarcane
rows.section_2_text::eng:
These foods help the body to grow, repair damaged tissues, and build muscles. 
They are very important for children, pregnant women, and people who are recovering from sickness.
rows.section_2_extra_text::eng:
* Groundnuts
* Beans
* Soya beans
* Sim sim
* Mushrooms
* Green grams
* Nuts

rows.section_1_text::eng: Protective foods help the body to fight diseases and maintain good health. They supply vitamins and minerals that strengthen the immune system and keep the body functioning properly.
rows.section_1_extra_text::eng:
* Vegetables
* Oranges
* Amaranthus 
* Watermelon
* Kales 
* Cabbage 
* Spinach
* Avocado
* Ripe bananas
* Pumpkin
* Onions
* Tomatoes
* Beverages, eg, coffee, tea

rows.text_1::eng: Some groups of people require special types of food because their bodies are growing, weak, or recovering from illness. Providing the right food helps improve their health and overall well-being.
rows.section_1_text::eng: Young children need soft and nutritious foods to support rapid growth and development. Breast milk is essential during the first six months. After that, other foods are gradually introduced to provide additional nutrients.
rows.section_1_extra_text::eng:
* Breast milkPorridge
* Eggs
* Mashed potatoes
* Fruits
rows.section_2_text::eng: Elderly people may have difficulty chewing or digesting food, so they require soft and easily digestible meals to maintain good health and strong immunity.

rows.section_1_text::eng: Pregnant and nursing mothers need extra nutrients to support their own health and the development of the baby. They should eat foods rich in iron, proteins, and vitamins to stay strong and healthy.
rows.section_2_text::eng: Sick people need foods that are easy to eat and digest because their bodies are weak. These foods help them recover faster, while taking enough fluids helps keep the body hydrated and fight infections.
rows.section_2_extra_text::eng:
* Porridge
* Soup
* Mashed bananas
* Fruits
* Fluids

rows.section_1_text::eng:
* Eating well is important for maintaining good health and preventing diseases. A balanced diet should include different types of food so that the body receives all the nutrients it needs.

* Depending too much on one type of food, such as maize, can lead to a lack of important nutrients in the body. It is important to include a variety of foods such as grains, legumes, vegetables, fruits, and animal products in daily meals.

* Mixing foods correctly improves their nutritional value. For example, when preparing porridge, combining cereals with legumes helps provide both energy and proteins, making the meal more nutritious.

* Children need foods that support both growth and protection from diseases. Giving them a combination of body-building and protective foods helps them develop properly and remain healthy.
Drinking enough water and other fluids is important for all people, especially for children, sick individuals, and nursing mothers, as it helps the body function properly.

rows.text_1::eng: Good nutrition supports health, while poor habits harm it, thus understanding this enhances well-being.
rows.section_1_text::eng: Poor eating habits can lead to a lack of essential nutrients, making the body weak and unable to function properly.
rows.section_1_extra_text::eng: Eat regular, balanced meals that include a variety of foods to support good health.
rows.section_2_text::eng: Malnutrition occurs when the body does not get enough nutrients, leading to weakness and poor growth, especially in children.
rows.section_2_extra_text::eng: Ensure a balanced diet with enough nutrients to support proper growth and development.
rows.section_3_text::eng: Anaemia is caused by a lack of iron, reducing the blood’s ability to carry oxygen and causing tiredness and weakness.
rows.section_3_extra_text::eng: Eat iron-rich foods and seek medical advice, especially for pregnant women.
rows.section_4_text::eng: Craving non-food items like soil may indicate nutrient deficiency and can lead to infections.
rows.section_4_extra_text::eng: Avoid eating non-food items and seek medical help to address possible nutrient deficiencies.

rows.title::eng: Challenges Faced by Farmers and Their Solutions
rows.text_1::eng: Many farmers work hard to produce food, but they do not always make good profits.
rows.text_2::eng: This happens because of several challenges that affect the quality, quantity, and market value of their products.
rows.section_1_text::eng: Perishable products like milk, fruits, and vegetables can go bad quickly, causing losses.
rows.section_1_extra_text::eng: Use proper storage methods and simple preservation techniques such as cooling, drying, or processing to extend shelf life.
rows.section_2_text::eng: Selling immediately after harvest often results in low prices due to high market supply.
rows.section_2_extra_text::eng: Practice value addition, storage, or timing the market to sell when prices are better.

rows.title::eng: Challenges Faced by Farmers and Their Solutions
rows.section_1_text::eng: Crops and livestock can be damaged, lowering both quality and quantity.
rows.section_1_extra_text::eng: Apply good farm management practices, including pest control and regular monitoring.
rows.section_2_text::eng: Farmers may struggle to find reliable buyers who offer fair prices.
rows.section_2_extra_text::eng: Join farmer groups or cooperatives to access better and more stable markets.

rows.title::eng: Challenges Faced by Farmers and Their Solutions
rows.section_1_text::eng: Growing large amounts of the same crop can flood the market and lower prices.
rows.section_1_extra_text::eng: Diversify production by growing different crops or engaging in other farm activities.

rows.section_1_text::eng: Value addition helps farmers earn more by selling products at higher prices instead of raw produce.
rows.section_1_extra_text::eng: Milk can be processed into yoghurt, which sells at a higher price.
rows.section_2_title::eng: Improves shelf life and reduces losses
rows.section_2_text::eng: Processing and preservation allow products to last longer without spoiling.
rows.section_2_extra_text::eng: Fish can be dried or smoked, and vegetables can be dried for future use.

rows.section_1_text::eng: Well-prepared and packaged products are more appealing to customers and easier to sell.
rows.section_1_extra_text::eng: Groundnuts can be roasted and made into peanut butter, which is more attractive than raw nuts.
rows.section_2_text::eng: Value addition allows farmers to turn one product into many different products.
rows.section_2_extra_text::eng: Soya beans can be processed into flour, milk, or snacks, while bananas can be made into crisps, flour, or juice.

rows.section_1_text::eng: Processed products can be sold in different places and at better prices.
rows.section_1_extra_text::eng: Cassava can be milled into flour, and sweet potatoes can be made into crisps for wider markets.

rows.text_1::eng: Maintaining quality and safety is essential in value addition, as it builds customer trust and improves market success.
rows.text_2::eng: To achieve this, it is important to follow good practices when handling your products.
rows.section_1_text::eng: * Using fresh and healthy raw materials ensures that the final product is safe and of good quality. Avoid using spoiled or damaged produce.
* Cleanliness during processing is essential. Always keep your hands, tools, and working environment clean to prevent contamination.
rows.section_2_text::eng: * Proper sorting helps remove damaged or unwanted items, improving the overall quality of the final product.

rows.section_1_text::eng: * Good storage protects products from moisture, pests, and contamination. Always store products in clean and dry containers.
rows.section_2_text::eng: * Attractive and proper packaging makes products easier to sell and gives customers confidence in their quality.
